CODEINE.mechanism: CYP2D6 converts codeine into morphine. Poor metabolizers have reduced analgesia while ultrarapid metabolizers risk toxicity.

CLOPIDOGREL.mechanism: CYP2C19 activates clopidogrel. Reduced function leads to decreased platelet inhibition.

WARFARIN.mechanism: CYP2C9 affects metabolism and VKORC1 affects drug sensitivity, influencing dose requirements.

SIMVASTATIN.mechanism: Reduced SLCO1B1 transporter function increases statin levels and myopathy risk.

AZATHIOPRINE.mechanism: Reduced TPMT or NUDT15 activity increases thiopurine toxicity risk.

CAPECITABINE.mechanism: DPYD deficiency reduces fluoropyrimidine metabolism leading to toxicity.

FLUOROURACIL.mechanism: DPYD deficiency reduces fluoropyrimidine metabolism leading to toxicity.
